creator: jhb@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hmm, I missed the followups earlier.  I was just able to apply the patch to an 
8.1 tree cleanly:

> patch < ~/work/patches/pci_ht.patch 
Hmm...  Looks like a unified diff to me...
The text leading up to this was:
--------------------------
|Index: pci.c
|===================================================================
|--- pci.c      (revision 219713)
|+++ pci.c      (working copy)
--------------------------
Patching file pci.c using Plan A...
Hunk #1 succeeded at 232 (offset -5 lines).
Hunk #2 succeeded at 579 (offset -34 lines).
Hunk #3 succeeded at 637 (offset -5 lines).
Hunk #4 succeeded at 620 (offset -34 lines).
Hunk #5 succeeded at 704 (offset -7 lines).
Hunk #6 succeeded at 1882 (offset -31 lines).
Hmm...  The next patch looks like a unified diff to me...
The text leading up to this was:
--------------------------
|Index: pcivar.h
|===================================================================
|--- pcivar.h   (revision 219713)
|+++ pcivar.h   (working copy)
--------------------------
Patching file pcivar.h using Plan A...
Hunk #1 succeeded at 110.
done

Perhaps the whitespace was corrupted in transmission?  Try fetching it from 
http://www.FreeBSD.org/~jhb/patches/pci_ht.patch

-- 
John Baldwin
